close_reason: OS_LOCKING_OK is the standard PKCS#11 initialization flag. All major PKCS#11 libraries support it. Runtime assertion would require querying C_GetInfo which adds complexity for no practical benefit.
description:
Trigger: a non-conformant or buggy PKCS#11 .so loaded that does not honor CKF_OS_LOCKING_OK and is not internally thread-safe.

Maintenance consequence: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:94 calls initialize(CInitializeArgs::new(CInitializeFlags::OS_LOCKING_OK)). If the library accepts this flag but is silently broken, concurrent sign() calls from multiple tasks (which the spawn_blocking architecture enables) can corrupt session state, return wrong signatures, or panic in the C library. The Rust code has no Mutex around session use because cryptoki sessions are independent per thread when the library is thread-safe.

This is a class of bug that produces 'works on softhsm but signs wrong on vendor X' issues, which are very hard to diagnose because the failure can be intermittent.

Action: (a) document that secretx-pkcs11 requires a thread-safe PKCS#11 library; list known-good libraries (SoftHSM2, pkcs11-proxy, AWS CloudHSM); (b) consider adding an opt-in 'serialize all PKCS#11 calls' mode via a Mutex<()> field for users with non-thread-safe libraries; (c) add a runtime check via C_GetInfo for the CKF_LIBRARY_CANT_CREATE_OS_THREADS flag and warn if present.
title: OS_LOCKING_OK assumes thread-safe library; no fallback or runtime assertion if ignored
